title: Discontinued Operations — Net of Tax
event: Ingle Co. reports income from continuing operations of $240,000 (after tax). Its discontinued segment produced a pretax operating loss of $60,000 and a pretax loss on disposal of $40,000. The tax rate is 25%. Compute the discontinued operations amount reported and net income.

computationPaths.narration: Discontinued operations get their own net-of-tax line below continuing operations — intraperiod tax allocation keeps each section carrying its own tax.

memorize.body: Discontinued ops = (operating results ± disposal gain/loss) × (1 − tax rate), shown as ONE net-of-tax line.
A loss brings a tax SAVING — the reported loss is smaller than pretax. Showing the gross 100,000 is the classic error.

questions.prompt: Pretax discontinued losses total $100,000, tax rate 25%. Amount reported for discontinued operations = ?

distractors.feedback: That's pretax. Discontinued operations are reported NET of tax: 100,000 × 75% = 75,000 loss.
That's the tax effect alone. The reported line is the loss AFTER that saving: 75,000.
